text: 🇺🇸 The man who spent years whining that the government illegally spied on his campaign is now demanding Congress let him spy on every American—without a warrant. Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇪🇺 🇫🇷 🇺🇦 According to Intelligence Online, the European consortium MBDA is likely assisting Ukraine in the development and improvement of FP-5 “Flamingo” cruise missiles. ▪️ The FP-5 incorporates key Western components, including inertial navigation systems and jam-resistant GPS modules similar to those used in Storm Shadow missiles. ▪️ Engineers with experience in MBDA structures have helped rapidly adapt the missile’s digital control system for real-time integration with intelligence data, while also resolving a number of initial technical issues. ▪️ The FP-5 can be seen as part of broader French-Ukrainian industrial integration. ▪️ This is further reflected in France’s interest in developing its own аналог under the “Chorus” project, involving companies such as Renault and Turgis Gaillard. WarMonitor If Iran drags this out, the current war runs into a hard deadline. Under the War Powers Resolution, Trump can’t legally continue military operations in Iran past 60 days without Congress. But he may try to redefine the operation to reset the clock if push comes to shove. Under the War Powers Act of 1973, presidents must terminate military operations after 60 days unless Congress has voted to declare war or passed legislation to authorize the use of force. https://time.com/article/2026/04/14/republicans-face-crucial-test-on-iran-war-as-60-day-deadline-looms/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=editorial&utm_content=140426 - TIME tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷The U.S. Treasury announced that they’re aggressively moving forward with Economic Fury. They advised financial institutions that they will be leveraging the full range of tools they have to deploy secondary sanctions against financial institutions…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇨🇳 🇦🇪 🇴🇲 NBC: The U.S. Treasury has warned that Iran processed around $9 billion in transactions in 2024 through front companies using banks mainly in Hong Kong and the United Arab Emirates. The U.S. Treasury sent a letter to China China, Hong Kong, the UAE, and Oman informing that U.S. intellgence found that Iran is able to bypass sanctions through front companies, shadow banking, deceptive practices, and permissive conduits. They urged authorities to identify and halt any related financial flows, warning that the U.S. could take enforcement action. The following are excerpts of the letter: "Despite the comprehensive sanctions that the United States has imposed on Iran, the regime continues to exploit the international financial system through front companies, shadow banking, deceptive practices, and permissive conduits. The Treasury Department encourages you to work with banks in your jurisdiction to identify any Iran-related financial activity, including through front companies and other evasive tactics, and immediately cease such activity in light of the significant illicit finance risks this activity poses. These include enforcement actions for sanctions violations that may involve a U.S. person; secondary sanctions against foreign financial institutions for certain activities with Iran; and the ability to require enhanced due diligence and reporting on all transactions of a defined scope that are processed through the U.S. financial system. Now is the time to finally disable Iran’s ability to support terrorism, threaten the region and global markets, and seek to continue its nuclear and ballistic missile program, which the U.N. has prohibited. My hope is for your swift action to identify and stop any illicit activity linked to Iran to avoid further action from Treasury." @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡ 🇮🇶 🇫🇷 🇮🇷 French Air Force Rafale fighter jets deployed in Iraq shot down at least two Iranian made Shahed-136 drones which were targeting Iranian-Kurdish opposition bases in the town of Koya. A higher resolution picture of one of the fighters shows its loadout. Three large external fuel tanks suggest that the aircraft was on an air patrol rather than scrambled from Erbil when it was tasked with the interception. A targeting pod can also be seen, used to improve detection and tracking of low flying and small targets such as drones. The aircrafts appear to be equipped with a pair of MICA air-to-air missiles. Maybe more were fired or are not visible. @wfwitness

🇱🇧 🇮🇱 𝗟𝗘𝗕𝗔𝗡𝗢𝗡–𝗜𝗦𝗥𝗔𝗘𝗟 𝗗𝗜𝗥𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗧𝗔𝗟𝗞𝗦: 𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗕𝗘𝗧𝗥𝗔𝗬𝗔𝗟 𝗢𝗙 𝗔 𝗡𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡 After years of struggle, resistance, blood, and martyrs, Lebanon now stands under a leadership imposed by the United States and Saudi Arabia, one that operates in alignment with Israel’s interests inside the country. The image emerging from the direct contacts and negotiations between Lebanon and “Israel”, conducted through ambassadors in Washington, does not reflect diplomacy, it reflects submission. A government and leadership that trade away rights, land, and dignity, offering concession after concession with nothing in return, only deeper losses carved into Lebanese sovereignty and paid for with the blood of its people. Such a government is not worthy of speaking on behalf of the majority of Lebanese, many of whom reject any form of negotiations or normalization with Israel. History will not be kind. President Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, in this view, will be remembered not as statesmen, but as figures who presided over the erosion of what generations fought and died to protect. These are not political compromises, they are seen as transactions made at the expense of a nation’s sacrifices. No matter how far these negotiations go, they do not decide the outcome. That is written elsewhere. On the ground, in the field, where the balance has always been shaped. There the decisive force remains the resistance, still standing, still fighting, still bearing the cost, refusing to surrender, refusing to abandon its people, and determined to prevent the enemy from ever imposing its will on Lebanese soil. Via: Ibrahim Majed on X 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇱🇾 🇮🇹 🇺🇸 The U.S.’s AFRICOM announced Libya, Italy, and the United States Exercise Flintlock 2026 in Libya, a multinational special forces operations exercise with forces from more than 30 nations across Côte d’Ivoire and Libya. The exercise will include coordinated air, ground, and maritime training aimed at improving combat readiness and operational cooperation among participating forces. Libya’s role in hosting part of the exercise is described as a milestone, with its forces facilitating training for over ten nations and signalling a commitment to regional security, while Italy has played a key role in planning and support. @wfwitness

The Trump administration has eased financial sanctions on Venezuela to help stabilize its struggling economy and address protests over low wages. The change allows major state banks, including the Banco Central de Venezuela, to use U.S. dollars, access oil revenues, and reconnect with the global financial system. Source: Axios

The IDF said the circumstances of the incident are under review. The battalion commander was evacuated to hospital and his family has been notified. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇨🇳 🇺🇸 Reuters: Eric Trump, son of U.S. President Trump, will accompany his father on a trip to China next month in a personal capacity, with his wife Lara. The move has raised concerns about a possible conflict of interest, given that Trump's personal wealth and business dealings are managed by Eric and other members of his family. However, Eric has said he has no business interests in China The trip is aimed at maintaining stable relations between Beijing and Washington, with the two countries having agreed to a truce last year after a period marked by Trump's tariffs and China's restrictions on rare-earth exports. @wfwitness

There is U.S. support for the Lebanese government’s plans to monopolize arms, and Washington hopes the talks will lead to a comprehensive peace agreement. Any Lebanese–Israeli ceasefire agreement will be under U.S. sponsorship and not through any separate track. The negotiations could lead to reconstruction and economic recovery for Lebanon. Israel supports the disarmament and dismantling of the infrastructure of armed groups in Lebanon, and it is committed to working with the Lebanese government on disarmament in a way that ensures the security of both countries. Lebanon affirms the principles of its territorial unity and its full sovereignty over its territory.” @wfwitness
